 Introduction and Objective: NA-931 is a first-in-class, orally administered small-molecule quadruple receptor agonist targeting IGF-1, GLP-1, GIP, and glucagon receptors, developed for the treatment of obesity and type 2 diabetes. This Phase 3 study evaluates the safety and efficacy of NA-931 as monotherapy and in combination with oral semaglutide for the treatment of obesity.

title: 2649-P: Population-Normed Z-scores for Body Composition Enhance Sensitivity and Effect Size: Analysis of a Phase 2 RCT Testing Anti-GDF8 and Anti-Activin A on Top of Semaglutide (COURAGE)
sectionPath: Poster Presentations: Obesity/Integrated Physiology > P: Obesity—Human
abstractSnippet:
Open figure viewer 
 Introduction and Objective: Body composition depends on weight, age, sex, height, and race, confounding comparisons across weight-loss interventions. A population-norming approach can provide weight, age, height, sex, and race-corrected z-scores for total lean mass (TLM) and fat mass (TFM). abstractSnippet:
Open figure viewer 
 Introduction and Objective: Adjunct therapy with non-insulin glucose-lowering agents is an attractive therapeutic strategy in type 1 diabetes (T1D) but carries risks of hypoglycemia and ketosis. We assessed the efficacy and safety of adding semaglutide weekly injection and oral dapagliflozin in with insulin therapy in overweight (BMI > 25 kg/m2) adults with T1D and HbA1c 7.5 -

title: 1683-P: Fracture Risk Associated with Tirzepatide: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials and Real-World Evidence
sectionPath: Poster Presentations: Clinical Diabetes/Therapeutics > P: Clinical Therapeutics—Incretin-Based Therapies
abstractSnippet:
Open figure viewer 
 Introduction and Objective: The impact of tirzepatide is substantial in reducing glucose and body weight, but its relevance to skeletal safety remains unknown. The purpose of this analysis is to evaluate the fracture risk of tirzepatide compared with controls in randomized trials and GLP-1 receptor agonists in real-world data.
